subject: 🏃 Wednesday — Mobility Deep-Dive
html:
Today: Mobility Deep-Dive — Week 2 of Chassis Build. This is your dedicated 35-minute session targeting bilateral hip internal rotation, adductors, hip flexors, and T-spine. It's also your third weekly hit on the PT-prescribed core work (dead bug, chin tucks). Knee has been solidly Green since the chassis phase started on Apr 20 — five straight sessions with zero complaints. That's exactly the trend we want heading into tomorrow's 4-miler, your first volume bump this cycle.

Focus cue: On the seated 90/90 flow, add a slow forward fold over the front knee on each side — that's where the IR stretch lives. Don't force depth; just breathe into the end range and let gravity do the work. Equal time both sides, no cheating the tight one.

Context: Week 2 of 4 in Chassis Build — 16 days to your May 15 PT follow-up, 88 days to race day. Tomorrow you step up to 4 miles for the first time this cycle. This session primes those hips for it.

Session — ~35 min:

  1. Foam roll full lower body — 5 min (IT band, TFL, glutes, quads, adductors)
  2. Foam roller T-spine extensions (PT) — 10 reps
  3. Quadruped banded hip IR / lateral band pull (PT) — 8 reps each side
  4. Standing lunges with hip IR + extension (PT) — 10 reps each side
  5. Seated 90/90 flow — 5 min (switch sides every 30 sec, add forward fold over front knee)
  6. Couch stretch — 90 sec each side
  7. Pigeon pose — 90 sec each side
  8. Banded hip distraction — 2 min each side
  9. Standing hip CARs — 5 each direction, each leg
  10. Quadruped thread-the-needle — 10 each side
  11. Dead bug — slow march with banded pulldown (PT) — 2 sets × 10 marches
  12. Chin tuck + lifts (PT) — 2 reps each direction (front, diag L, diag R), 3-sec hold

Equipment needed: Foam roller, light resistance band, something to anchor the band for hip distraction. That's it — no dumbbells today.
